questions.q: What must you do BEFORE a `nodeSelector` can match a node?
options: Add a taint to the node
Label the node with the required key-value pair
Add a toleration to the pod
Create a LimitRange in the namespace
questions.explanation: nodeSelector matches against node labels. You must first label the target node (`kubectl label nodes <node> <key>=<value>`) before the selector can work.

questions.q: A pod has `nodeSelector: {disk: ssd}` but no nodes have the label `disk=ssd`. What happens?
options: The pod runs on any available node
The pod is rejected immediately with an error
The pod stays in Pending state indefinitely
The scheduler assigns the pod to the node with the most free memory
questions.explanation: If no nodes match the nodeSelector, the pod cannot be scheduled and stays in Pending. It will remain there until a matching node becomes available or the pod is deleted.

questions.q: Which of the following scheduling requirements CANNOT be expressed using `nodeSelector`?
options: Schedule only on nodes with label disk=ssd
Schedule only on nodes with label env=production
Schedule on nodes with label size=large OR size=xlarge
Schedule only on nodes with label region=us-east
questions.explanation: nodeSelector only supports exact equality matches. OR conditions (e.g., size=large OR size=xlarge) require Node Affinity with the 'In' operator and a list of values.

questions.q: `nodeSelector` supports the NotIn operator to exclude nodes with specific labels.
questions.explanation: False. nodeSelector only supports simple equality-based matching (key=value). Operators like NotIn, Exists, or DoesNotExist require Node Affinity (matchExpressions).
